content.problemStatement:
Chef has a circular pizza and a knife to cut that into pieces.
He can only cut the pizza in a way such that the knife starts from the boundary of the pizza, passes the centre, and ends at the boundary.

Find whether Chef an divide the pizza into NNN pieces using any (possibly zero) number of cuts.

Some valid cuts are:

Whereas, the following cuts are invalid:


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single integer NNN — denoting the desired number of cuts.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, `YES`, if Chef can divide the pizza into NNN pieces using any (possibly zero) number of cuts, and `NO` otherwise.


Note that you may print each character in uppercase or lowercase. For example, the strings `NO`, `no`, `No`, and `nO` are considered the same.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N, the desired number of pizza pieces.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line containing "YES" if Chef can obtain exactly N pieces using any number of valid cuts, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: N=1 → zero cuts → 1 piece (YES). N=2 → one diameter → 2 pieces (YES). N=3 is odd and >1 → impossible (NO). N=4 → two diameters → 4 pieces (YES).
5 and 7 are odd >1 → NO. 6 is even → YES.
1 is always possible. 1000 is even, so possible.

solution.approach:
Each valid cut is a diameter of the circle. With 0 cuts we have 1 piece. Every additional diameter splits the circle into two more pieces because all diameters intersect only at the centre. Therefore the number of pieces obtainable is:
- 1 piece when no cuts are made,
- 2 * k pieces when k >= 1 cuts are made.
Thus the reachable numbers are exactly 1 and all even numbers >= 2. For each N we simply check if N == 1 or N is even.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
